title:
Commentary on Membership
(Members data presented are as of December 31, 2026)
content:
CAMPUS reported 44 current members as of December 31, 2026. Membership levels remained flat during the period, with no change from the previous year-end but increasing 2% on a quarter-over-quarter basis. Potential membership totaled 156, remaining unchanged and providing a stable base for future membership development initiatives.

Operational efficiency metrics remain consistent. Members per full-time employee have fluctuated over recent quarters, showing variability in staffing alignment with service demand. This pattern indicates that while overall ratios are within expected ranges, there is ongoing adjustment to match operational requirements with member volume.

Shares per member continued to rise during the period, reflecting a strengthening of deposit balances and improved funding stability among members. While this trend reflects positive member engagement and retention efforts, it underscores the importance of maintaining competitive rates and service offerings to support further growth in share ownership.

Overall, membership trends remain stable with modest quarterly gains. The institution retains meaningful opportunity to expand within its existing potential membership base. Continued focus on member engagement, product penetration, and service accessibility will remain important to supporting sustainable membership growth and long-term balance-sheet development.
